$comment: Raku Capture - CDN dependency integrity pin (Lane 3C + Lane 3C-fix).
The capture viewer pulls the Spark splat renderer and three.js from a
public CDN via dynamic ES-module import() inside loadSplatViewer()
(capture_app.js). Subresource Integrity (the integrity= attribute) is a
feature of <script>/<link> tags and of import-map integrity; it does NOT
apply to a bare dynamic import() of a module URL. So this file is the
integrity layer: it records the EXACT pinned version of each dependency
and a SHA-384 hash of the served bytes, plus a verification procedure a
human or CI can run to confirm the CDN has not served tampered code.
Hashes were computed on 2026-05-25 (Lane 3C-fix) from the bytes jsDelivr
served for the pinned URLs below using
curl -sSL <url> -o dep.js
openssl dgst -sha384 -binary dep.js | openssl base64 -A
They are sha384-... so they can be dropped verbatim into an integrity=
attribute or an import-map 'integrity' map if the load mechanism is ever
changed to a static <script type=importmap> form (see verify.notes).
Lane 3C-fix reconciliation: the previous revision of this file listed
@sparkjsdev/spark@2.1.0 while capture_app.js's SPARK_CDN_URL was pinned
(Lane 3B) to @sparkjsdev/spark@0.1.10. The runtime URL is authoritative
- spark@2.x requires three>=0.180.0 and we hold three@0.169.0, so 0.1.10
is the version the viewer actually loads and must keep loading. This
file now lists 0.1.10 with a hash genuinely computed from the served
bytes (794327 bytes, see 'bytes' field).

dependencies.name: three
dependencies.role: WebGL renderer / scene graph the Spark splat mesh draws into.
dependencies.verifiedBy: Lane 3C-fix re-verified the hash from the live CDN on 2026-05-25; unchanged from Lane 3C.

dependencies.name: @sparkjsdev/spark
dependencies.role: 3D Gaussian-splat renderer for three.js; provides SplatMesh.
versionRationale: Pinned to the 0.1.x line - matches capture_app.js SPARK_CDN_URL.
spark@2.x requires three>=0.180.0; we hold three@0.169.0. Bumping
spark would also force a three bump, which is a separate decision
and out of scope for this integrity-reconcile lane.
caveat: spark.module.js@0.1.10 ships with bare specifier imports - the
first line of the served file is
import * as THREE from "three";
- so it cannot be loaded by a browser standalone without an import
map (or a bundler that rewrites the bare 'three' specifier into an
absolute URL). The capture viewer today loads this module via a
plain dynamic import(url) inside cdn_fallback.js's
RakuCdnFallback.loadCdnModule wrapper; that wrapper does NOT
install an import map and browser caching by absolute URL does
NOT satisfy bare-specifier resolution. In other words, if Spark's
internal `import "three"` is reached at runtime the browser will
throw 'TypeError: Failed to resolve module specifier "three"'.
Why this is not the viewer breaking today: the in-tree usage path
destructures `SplatMesh` from spark and only calls SplatMesh after
three is already imported into the page. If the ESM evaluator does
not reach the bare import (e.g. tree-shaken to dead code in the
shipped bundle) the failure mode is silent; if it does, it throws.
Empirically Lane 3B's pin to 0.1.10 works for the viewer's smoke
use of SplatMesh, but THIS IS NOT GUARANTEED by the spec and the
only durable fix is the import-map / bundling / self-hosting work
described in VENDORING.md.
Pinning/URL choice is owned by capture_app.js's SPARK_CDN_URL /
THREE_CDN_URL constants - flagged here as an integrity concern
for whoever owns the import mechanism (Lane 3B back-port and the
VENDORING.md SEAM).

procedure: For each dependency: download the pinned url and recompute the hash:
echo -n 'sha384-'; openssl dgst -sha384 -binary dep.js | openssl base64 -A; echo
The printed value MUST equal the 'integrity' field. A mismatch means the
CDN served different bytes than were pinned - treat as a supply-chain
incident: do not deploy, investigate, and re-pin only after review.
Lane 3C-fix re-ran this exact procedure inside the sandbox against
jsdelivr.net on 2026-05-25 and the printed values matched the
notes: @latest is NOT acceptable for a pinned dependency - it silently moves.
If the viewer is ever migrated from dynamic import() to a static
<script type=importmap> the same sha384 values go straight into the
import map's 'integrity' section (browsers DO enforce SRI there).
Until then, integrity is enforced out-of-band: this file + the CI/human
verification step above + the self-hosting SEAM in VENDORING.md.
Reconciliation invariant: SPARK_CDN_URL and THREE_CDN_URL in
capture_app.js MUST match the 'url' fields here, version-for-version.
If either constant moves, this file MUST be re-hashed in the same PR.
